title: LilyGO first hardware target bring-up
description: Why this exists: R1/R1 Neo is the product-shaped target, but physical R1 smoke testing is blocked. LilyGO-class Meshtastic-compatible hardware should be the first practical live bring-up/reference target because it is easier to obtain, better documented, and useful for contributors. What needs to be done: select the exact first LilyGO board, confirm hardware facts from sources, add or adapt Zephyr board support and app overlays, validate radio/BLE/USB/serial/display/input/GNSS/power boundaries through the LICHEN HAL, flash real hardware, run packet-path and app-interface smoke tests, and document limitations. Acceptance criteria: exact LilyGO board and revision are named; board facts and source links are recorded; Zephyr board target/overlay builds; firmware flashes and boots on physical hardware; LoRa radio smoke passes at minimum TX/RX or loopback with known peer; BLE/native-client or serial LCI smoke path is verified; display/input status UI is verified or explicitly split to project-LICHEN-rjsw; unresolved hardware gaps are filed as follow-up Beads; code changes have three codereview passes before implementation Beads close.
notes:
Positioning decision: R1/R1 Neo remains the product acceptance target, but LilyGO is the first practical live bring-up/reference board. Board-specific details must stay under Zephyr board files, devicetree overlays, Kconfig defaults, and HAL capability descriptors; protocol, native app, Meshtastic, MeshCore, and TUI logic must remain board-agnostic.
Scope clarification from 2026-06-30: first LilyGO hardware target is LilyGO T-Deck because user has one available. Other LilyGO targets remain secondary until T-Deck build/flash/radio/local-client/UI smoke path is understood.
No-hardware loop block 2026-07-02: requires physical LilyGO/T-Deck hardware access for display/input, flashing, boot logs, packet/app smoke tests, or full bring-up acceptance. Software-only board/model/build slices are already split into completed or separate Beads; resume when a physical board and flashing/serial access are available.
